meaning: adj.恃强凌弱的,虚张声势的
examples: The swashbuckling skills will serve her well in King Arthur and the Pirates trilogy.
But the gold hoops weren't just swashbuckling fashion statements - they served several useful purposes.
Marvelous moustaches and swashbuckling sideburns packed out this year’s Alpine Beard Festival in Chur, Switzerland.
With that armoire full of frocks and furbelows, he's as much swish-buckling as swashbuckling.
The American club bar and the local hotels seethed with spies, swashbuckling journalists and camera crews.
